openclaw / openclaw 卡住 / gateway 没响应"等需要操控 daemon 进程时，必读此 skill。
说明哪些 CLI 不能用、以及正确的恢复路径。

entries.name: kimi-webbridge
entries.description: Kimi WebBridge lets AI control the user's real browser — navigate, click, type, read, screenshot, and interact with any website using the user's actual login sessions. Use this skill whenever the user wants to interact with websites, automate browser tasks, scrape web content, or perform any action requiring a real browser. Also use when the user mentions "browser", "webpage", "open URL", "screenshot", or asks to read/interact with any website. Use even for simple-sounding browser requests — the daemon handles all complexity.

entries.name: kimiim-cli
entries.description: Use this skill for any interaction with Kimi Group Chat or its Sessions, including reading Group Rules, checking members and recent messages, replying in group/thread context, and handling Kimi IM files. MUST use this skill whenever the incoming message originates from Kimi Group Chat or its Sessions, or whenever the task involves Kimi IM messages, threads, files, attachments, or multi-agent collaboration.

data.prompt: [Fri 2026-05-22 22:51 CDT] did the .md gaps file land?

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

tools.name: exec
tools.description: Execute shell commands with background continuation for work that starts now. Use yieldMs/background to continue later via process tool. For long-running work started now, rely on automatic completion wake when it is enabled and the command emits output or fails; otherwise use process to confirm completion. Use process whenever you need logs, status, input, or intervention. Use pty=true for TTY-required commands (terminal UIs, coding agents).

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, kills process on expiry)

pty.description: Run in a pseudo-terminal (PTY) when available (TTY-required CLIs, coding agents)

elevated.description: Run on the host with elevated permissions (if allowed)

security.description: Ignored for normal calls; exec security is set by tools.exec.security and host approvals.

tools.name: image
tools.description: Analyze images with vision model. Use image for one path/URL, images for max 20. Only use this tool when images were NOT already provided; prompt images already visible.

tools.name: image_generate
tools.description: Create/edit images. Session chats: background task; do not call image_generate again for same request; wait completion, then send attachments via message tool. Transparent: outputFormat="png" or "webp" + background="transparent"; OpenAI also supports openai.background and routes default model to gpt-image-1.5. Use action="list" for providers/models/readiness/auth, "status" for active task.

# Cockpit → Control UI Migration Gap Analysis & Build Spec

> **Status**: Cockpit is a standalone generic `/v1` chat client. This doc maps what must be built to reach functional parity with the built-in OpenClaw Control UI, while preserving Cockpit's design language and theme tokens.

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Cockpit (current) is **not** a Control UI replacement. It is a lightweight, dark-first chat client for generic OpenAI-compatible endpoints. The Control UI (built-in) is a full Gateway management dashboard with server-side chat semantics.

**Goal**: Bridge the gap by layering Control UI capabilities *into* the Cockpit shell, using Cockpit's existing oklch-based theme system, component architecture (shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4), and TanStack Router structure.

---

## 2. What's Missing vs. Control UI

### 🔴 Critical — Gateway Management (Zero Parity)

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Gateway WebSocket** | Direct WS auth, chat semantics (`chat.send`, `chat.history`, `chat.abort`, `chat.inject`) | ❌ HTTP-only generic client | High |
| **Config Editor** | `config.get`, `config.set`, `config.apply`, schema-driven forms, raw JSON editor, base-hash guards | ❌ None | High |
| **Live Logs Tail** | Real-time gateway log streaming | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Debug Panel** | Status/health snapshots, manual RPC calls, event log | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Cron Jobs** | List/add/edit/run/enable/disable + run history (`cron.*`) | ❌ None | High |
| **Skills** | Install, enable/disable, API key updates (`skills.*`) | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Nodes** | List + capabilities (`node.list`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Exec Approvals** | Edit gateway/node allowlists | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Channels** | QR login, per-channel config, probe refreshes | ❌ None | High |
| **Instances/Presence** | Live presence list (`system-presence`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Sessions** | Session picker, per-session overrides (model/thinking/verbose/trace/reasoning) | ❌ None | High |
| **Dreams/Diary** | Dreaming status, diary reader (`doctor.memory.*`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Update/Restart** | Package/git update + restart polling | ❌ None | Medium |

### 🟡 Important — Chat Experience Gaps

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Server-Side Chat** | Bounded history, directive stripping, optimistic tail merge, oversized message handling | ❌ LocalStorage-only threads | High |
| **Slash Commands** | `/new`, `/reset`, `/stop` interception with semantic routing | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Tool Streaming** | Live tool output cards, agent events | ❌ None | High |
| **Voice/Talk** | WebRTC (OpenAI), WebSocket (Google Live), Gateway relay transport, VAD, mic streaming | ❌ Stub button (non-functional) | High |
| **Model Picker** | Dynamic provider catalogs, `agents.defaults.models` allowlist | ❌ Hardcoded text field | Medium |
| **Context Usage** | Token pressure indicator, compaction button | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Session Overrides** | Per-session model/thinking/fast/verbose/trace/reasoning toggles | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Images** | Managed media references, authenticated Gateway URLs | ❌ Base64 in localStorage (bloated) | Medium |

### 🟢 Present in Cockpit (Preserve)

| Feature | Notes |

# Cockpit → Control UI Migration Gap Analysis & Build Spec

> **Status**: Cockpit is a standalone generic `/v1` chat client. This doc maps what must be built to reach functional parity with the built-in OpenClaw Control UI, while preserving Cockpit's design language and theme tokens.

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Cockpit (current) is **not** a Control UI replacement. It is a lightweight, dark-first chat client for generic OpenAI-compatible endpoints. The Control UI (built-in) is a full Gateway management dashboard with server-side chat semantics.

**Goal**: Bridge the gap by layering Control UI capabilities *into* the Cockpit shell, using Cockpit's existing oklch-based theme system, component architecture (shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4), and TanStack Router structure.

---

## 2. What's Missing vs. Control UI

### 🔴 Critical — Gateway Management (Zero Parity)

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Gateway WebSocket** | Direct WS auth, chat semantics (`chat.send`, `chat.history`, `chat.abort`, `chat.inject`) | ❌ HTTP-only generic client | High |
| **Config Editor** | `config.get`, `config.set`, `config.apply`, schema-driven forms, raw JSON editor, base-hash guards | ❌ None | High |
| **Live Logs Tail** | Real-time gateway log streaming | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Debug Panel** | Status/health snapshots, manual RPC calls, event log | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Cron Jobs** | List/add/edit/run/enable/disable + run history (`cron.*`) | ❌ None | High |
| **Skills** | Install, enable/disable, API key updates (`skills.*`) | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Nodes** | List + capabilities (`node.list`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Exec Approvals** | Edit gateway/node allowlists | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Channels** | QR login, per-channel config, probe refreshes | ❌ None | High |
| **Instances/Presence** | Live presence list (`system-presence`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Sessions** | Session picker, per-session overrides (model/thinking/verbose/trace/reasoning) | ❌ None | High |
| **Dreams/Diary** | Dreaming status, diary reader (`doctor.memory.*`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Update/Restart** | Package/git update + restart polling | ❌ None | Medium |

### 🟡 Important — Chat Experience Gaps

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Server-Side Chat** | Bounded history, directive stripping, optimistic tail merge, oversized message handling | ❌ LocalStorage-only threads | High |
| **Slash Commands** | `/new`, `/reset`, `/stop` interception with semantic routing | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Tool Streaming** | Live tool output cards, agent events | ❌ None | High |
| **Voice/Talk** | WebRTC (OpenAI), WebSocket (Google Live), Gateway relay transport, VAD, mic streaming | ❌ Stub button (non-functional) | High |
| **Model Picker** | Dynamic provider catalogs, `agents.defaults.models` allowlist | ❌ Hardcoded text field | Medium |
| **Context Usage** | Token pressure indicator, compaction button | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Session Overrides** | Per-session model/thinking/fast/verbose/trace/reasoning toggles | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Images** | Managed media references, authenticated Gateway URLs | ❌ Base64 in localStorage (bloated) | Medium |

### 🟢 Present in Cockpit (Preserve)

| Feature | Notes |

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

tools.name: exec
tools.description: Execute shell commands with background continuation for work that starts now. Use yieldMs/background to continue later via process tool. For long-running work started now, rely on automatic completion wake when it is enabled and the command emits output or fails; otherwise use process to confirm completion. Use process whenever you need logs, status, input, or intervention. Use pty=true for TTY-required commands (terminal UIs, coding agents).

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, kills process on expiry)

pty.description: Run in a pseudo-terminal (PTY) when available (TTY-required CLIs, coding agents)

elevated.description: Run on the host with elevated permissions (if allowed)

security.description: Ignored for normal calls; exec security is set by tools.exec.security and host approvals.

tools.name: image
tools.description: Analyze images with vision model. Use image for one path/URL, images for max 20. Only use this tool when images were NOT already provided; prompt images already visible.

tools.name: image_generate
tools.description: Create/edit images. Session chats: background task; do not call image_generate again for same request; wait completion, then send attachments via message tool. Transparent: outputFormat="png" or "webp" + background="transparent"; OpenAI also supports openai.background and routes default model to gpt-image-1.5. Use action="list" for providers/models/readiness/auth, "status" for active task.

model.description: Provider/model override, e.g. openai/gpt-image-2; transparent OpenAI: openai/gpt-image-1.5.

filename.description: Output filename hint; basename preserved in managed media dir.

background.description: Background: transparent, opaque, auto. Transparent needs png/webp output.

tools.name: memory_get
tools.description: Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. Optional `corpus=wiki` or `corpus=all` also searches registered compiled-wiki supplements. `corpus=memory` restricts hits to indexed memory files (excludes session transcript chunks from ranking). `corpus=sessions` restricts hits to indexed session transcripts (same visibility rules as session history tools). If response has disabled=true, memory retrieval is unavailable and should be surfaced to the user.

tools.name: music_generate
tools.description: Create audio/music for song, jingle, beat, loop, soundtrack, anthem, instrumental requests. If user asks make/generate/create song/music, call music_generate; do not just write lyrics unless lyrics/text only. Prompt gets style/genre/mood/tempo/instruments/purpose. lyrics only exact sung words. Session chats: background task; do not call again for same request; wait completion, send attachments via message tool. "status" checks active task.

prompt.description: Music prompt: style, genre, mood, purpose.

lyrics.description: Exact sung lyrics only when the user supplies lyrics or asks for vocal words. For song/style requests, use prompt instead.

filename.description: Output filename hint; basename preserved in managed media dir.

tools.name: process
tools.description: Manage running exec sessions for commands already started: list, poll, log, write, send-keys, submit, paste, kill. Use poll/log when you need status, logs, quiet-success confirmation, or completion confirmation when automatic completion wake is unavailable. Use poll/log also for input-wait hints. Use write/send-keys/submit/paste/kill for input or intervention.

sessionId.description: Session id for actions other than list

keys.description: Key tokens to send for send-keys

hex.description: Hex bytes to send for send-keys

timeout.description: For poll: wait up to this many milliseconds before returning; max 30000 ms, higher values are clamped to 30000

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

tools.name: session_status
tools.description: Show /status-like card for current/visible session: model, usage, time, cost, tasks. Use `sessionKey="current"` for current session; UI labels like `openclaw-tui` are not keys. `model` sets session override; `model=default` resets. Use for active model/session config questions.

tools.name: sessions_history

tools.name: sessions_list
tools.description: List visible sessions; filter by kind, label, agentId, search, activity. Use before sessions_history or sessions_send target selection.

tools.name: sessions_yield
tools.description: End current turn. Use after spawning subagents; results arrive as next message.

tools.name: subagents
tools.description: List/kill/steer spawned subagents for requester session. If sessions_yield exists, use it for completion; do not poll wait loops.

tools.name: update_plan
tools.description: Update current run plan. Use for non-trivial multi-step work; keep plan current while executing. Short steps; max one `in_progress`; skip for simple one-step work.

tools.name: video_generate

imageRoles.description: `image` + `images` roles by index after de-dupe. Values: first_frame, last_frame, reference_image; empty string leaves unset.

videoRoles.description: `video` + `videos` roles by index after de-dupe. Value: reference_video; empty string leaves unset.

aspectRatio.description: Aspect ratio: 1:1, 16:9, 9:16, "adaptive", or provider value; unsupported normalized/ignored.

resolution.description: Resolution: 480P, 720P, 768P, 1080P, 4K, or provider value; unsupported normalized/ignored.

durationSeconds.description: Target seconds; may round to nearest supported duration.

providerOptions.description: Provider JSON options, e.g. {"seed":42}. Keys/types must match provider capabilities; mismatch skips candidate. Use action=list for accepted keys.

tools.name: web_fetch

tools.description: Search web for current info; returns normalized provider results.

tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

# Cockpit → Control UI Migration Gap Analysis & Build Spec

> **Status**: Cockpit is a standalone generic `/v1` chat client. This doc maps what must be built to reach functional parity with the built-in OpenClaw Control UI, while preserving Cockpit's design language and theme tokens.

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Cockpit (current) is **not** a Control UI replacement. It is a lightweight, dark-first chat client for generic OpenAI-compatible endpoints. The Control UI (built-in) is a full Gateway management dashboard with server-side chat semantics.

**Goal**: Bridge the gap by layering Control UI capabilities *into* the Cockpit shell, using Cockpit's existing oklch-based theme system, component architecture (shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4), and TanStack Router structure.

---

## 2. What's Missing vs. Control UI

### 🔴 Critical — Gateway Management (Zero Parity)

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Gateway WebSocket** | Direct WS auth, chat semantics (`chat.send`, `chat.history`, `chat.abort`, `chat.inject`) | ❌ HTTP-only generic client | High |
| **Config Editor** | `config.get`, `config.set`, `config.apply`, schema-driven forms, raw JSON editor, base-hash guards | ❌ None | High |
| **Live Logs Tail** | Real-time gateway log streaming | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Debug Panel** | Status/health snapshots, manual RPC calls, event log | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Cron Jobs** | List/add/edit/run/enable/disable + run history (`cron.*`) | ❌ None | High |
| **Skills** | Install, enable/disable, API key updates (`skills.*`) | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Nodes** | List + capabilities (`node.list`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Exec Approvals** | Edit gateway/node allowlists | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Channels** | QR login, per-channel config, probe refreshes | ❌ None | High |
| **Instances/Presence** | Live presence list (`system-presence`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Sessions** | Session picker, per-session overrides (model/thinking/verbose/trace/reasoning) | ❌ None | High |
| **Dreams/Diary** | Dreaming status, diary reader (`doctor.memory.*`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Update/Restart** | Package/git update + restart polling | ❌ None | Medium |

### 🟡 Important — Chat Experience Gaps

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Server-Side Chat** | Bounded history, directive stripping, optimistic tail merge, oversized message handling | ❌ LocalStorage-only threads | High |
| **Slash Commands** | `/new`, `/reset`, `/stop` interception with semantic routing | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Tool Streaming** | Live tool output cards, agent events | ❌ None | High |
| **Voice/Talk** | WebRTC (OpenAI), WebSocket (Google Live), Gateway relay transport, VAD, mic streaming | ❌ Stub button (non-functional) | High |
| **Model Picker** | Dynamic provider catalogs, `agents.defaults.models` allowlist | ❌ Hardcoded text field | Medium |
| **Context Usage** | Token pressure indicator, compaction button | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Session Overrides** | Per-session model/thinking/fast/verbose/trace/reasoning toggles | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Images** | Managed media references, authenticated Gateway URLs | ❌ Base64 in localStorage (bloated) | Medium |

### 🟢 Present in Cockpit (Preserve)

| Feature | Notes |

The problem: it tells an AI agent to **create new parallel implementations** (`lib/gateway-client.ts`, `lib/gateway-chat.ts`, `lib/config-store.ts`, etc.) rather than **importing and extending the Control UI's existing code**.

Key evidence:
- Section 5 lists **new files to create** (`lib/gateway-client.ts`, `routes/admin/config.tsx`, etc.) with no mention of importing from the built-in Control UI source
- Section 6 is phased **implementation**, not **integration**
- Section 8 lists raw RPC signatures as if the Cockpit needs its own client layer

**What's actually available to reuse**: The built-in Control UI is part of the OpenClaw gateway — its components, stores, and RPC handlers already exist in the gateway codebase. The Cockpit is a **separate static build** (`dist/client/`) that currently doesn't share code with the gateway.

**The real question**: Are you asking the Cockpit to:
1. **Consume the Control UI as a library** (import its React components/stores)?
2. **Embed the Control UI** (iframe or route proxy)?
3. **Just align visually** (same theme tokens, but separate code)?

The current MIGRATION-GAPS.md assumes #3 (visual alignment) + new code for everything else. If you want #1 or #2, the spec needs a rewrite.

```

### Layer 1: Gateway Bridge (New)

Add a **Gateway-aware communication layer** that coexists with the generic `/v1` client:

└── talk.tsx              # NEW: Voice/Talk interface
```

### Layer 3: Component Extensions (New)

```
components/cockpit/
├── Drawer.tsx            # EXISTING — add admin nav section

## 6. Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Foundation (Gateway Connection)

1. **Gateway WebSocket client** (`lib/gateway-client.ts`)
   - Auth: token, password, Tailscale identity headers
   - Reconnection with exponential backoff
   - Message framing: JSON-RPC or Gateway native protocol
   - Pairing flow: detect `1008: pairing required`, surface approval UI

2. **Theme system expansion** (`lib/theme-store.ts` + `styles.css`)
   - Add `themeMode: "dark" | "light" | "system"` to store
   - Light mode CSS overrides using inverted overlays
   - System mode via `matchMedia('prefers-color-scheme')`
   - Persist to localStorage

3. **Route shell extension** (`__root.tsx`)
   - Add admin navigation drawer section
   - Conditional rendering based on Gateway connection status

### Phase 2: Server-Side Chat

1. **Gateway chat adapter** (`lib/gateway-chat.ts`)
   - `chat.send` with session context
   - `chat.history` with bounded window
   - `chat.abort` with runId targeting
   - Directive stripping (`/new`, `/reset`, `/stop`)
   - Optimistic tail merging

2. **Unified chat interface** (`routes/index.tsx`)
   - Toggle: Generic `/v1` mode vs. Gateway mode
   - Display context usage indicator
   - Compaction button at high token pressure

3. **Message row enhancement** (`components/cockpit/ChatMessage.tsx`)
   - Tool output cards
   - Streaming indicators
   - Oversized message handling

### Phase 3: Admin Dashboard

1. **Config editor** (`routes/admin/config.tsx`)
   - Schema-driven form rendering (`config.schema.lookup`)
   - Raw JSON editor with base-hash guard
   - SecretRef resolution display (read-only)
   - Apply + restart flow

2. **Cron manager** (`routes/admin/cron.tsx`)
   - List jobs with schedule + payload preview
   - Add/edit: schedule builder (at/every/cron)
   - Run history with status
   - Enable/disable toggle

3. **Skills manager** (`routes/admin/skills.tsx`)

4. **Nodes + channels** (`routes/admin/nodes.tsx`, `routes/admin/channels.tsx`)
   - Node list with capabilities
   - Channel status + QR login
   - Exec approval editor

### Phase 4: Advanced Features

1. **Session management** (`routes/admin/sessions.tsx`)
   - Session list with metadata
   - Per-session overrides (model, thinking, fast, verbose, trace, reasoning)
   - Kill/steer actions

2. **Voice/Talk** (`routes/talk.tsx`)
   - WebRTC setup (OpenAI)
   - WebSocket setup (Google Live)
   - Gateway relay transport
   - Microphone controls + VAD visualization

3. **Logs + debug** (`routes/admin/logs.tsx`, `routes/admin/debug.tsx`)
   - WebSocket log tail
   - Manual RPC call interface
   - Status/health snapshots

4. **i18n framework**
   - Lazy-loaded locale bundles
   - 20 locale targets (match Control UI)

---

## 7. Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| **Coexist generic + Gateway modes** | Generic `/v1` mode is useful for external APIs; Gateway mode is for OpenClaw management |
| **localStorage as offline fallback** | Threads stored locally when Gateway unavailable; sync to server when connected |
| **oklch hue cycle as identity** | Unique visual signature; extends to light mode by adjusting brightness |
| **TanStack Router file routes** | Already in use; scales naturally for new admin routes |
| **shadcn/ui primitives** | Already have 48+ components; use for admin forms, not just chat |
| **WebSocket per feature** | Logs, chat, presence each may need separate WS channels or multiplex |

---

## 8. API Surface Needed

The Cockpit needs to call these Gateway RPCs (currently unavailable in the generic client):

```typescript
// Connection
connect(params: { auth: { token?: string; password?: string } })

// Chat
cchat.send(params: { sessionKey?: string; message: string; attachments?: string[] })
chat.history(params: { sessionKey?: string; limit?: number })
chat.abort(params: { sessionKey?: string; runId?: string })
chat.inject(params: { sessionKey?: string; text: string })

// Config
config.get()
config.set(params: { path: string; value: unknown; baseHash?: string })
config.apply(params: { restart?: boolean })
config.schema.lookup(params: { path?: string })

// Cron
cron.list()
cron.add(params: { job: CronJob })
cron.update(params: { jobId: string; patch: Partial<CronJob> })
cron.remove(params: { jobId: string })
cron.run(params: { jobId: string })
cron.runs(params: { jobId: string })

[50 more lines in file. Use offset=351 to continue.]

// Skills
skills.list()
skills.status(params: { skillId: string })
skills.enable(params: { skillId: string })
skills.disable(params: { skillId: string })

// Nodes
node.list()

// Presence
system-presence()

// Status/Debug
status()
health()
models.list(params: { view?: "all" | "configured" })
```

---

## 9. Risk Assessment

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| **WebSocket auth complexity** | Start with token auth; defer Tailscale/trusted-proxy to Phase 3 |
| **Config schema rendering** | Use existing shadcn/ui form components; schema walker generates fields |
| **Chat state synchronization** | Generic mode = localStorage only; Gateway mode = server source of truth |
| **Voice/WebRTC complexity** | Defer to Phase 4; stub with "coming soon" if needed earlier |
| **Build size bloat** | Code-split admin routes; lazy load Gateway client |

---

## 10. Success Criteria

- [ ] Gateway WebSocket connects with token auth
- [ ] Server-side chat replaces localStorage threads when connected
- [ ] Config editor renders schema forms + raw JSON
- [ ] Cron jobs CRUD functional
- [ ] Skills list + enable/disable works
- [ ] Theme toggles dark/light/system with oklch preserved
- [ ] All existing Cockpit features (generic `/v1`, local threads, images, library) still work
- [ ] PWA manifest + service worker re-added
- [ ] i18n framework ready for 20 locales

*Author: AI Agent — Cockpit Migration Analysis*

Looking at the full file now — **no, it's not hardened for that.** The spec is written as a *build-from-scratch* migration plan, not a *reuse-existing* plan.

The problem: it tells an AI agent to **create new parallel implementations** (`lib/gateway-client.ts`, `lib/gateway-chat.ts`, `lib/config-store.ts`, etc.) rather than **importing and extending the Control UI's existing code**.

Key evidence:
- Section 5 lists **new files to create** (`lib/gateway-client.ts`, `routes/admin/config.tsx`, etc.) with no mention of importing from the built-in Control UI source
- Section 6 is phased **implementation**, not **integration**
- Section 8 lists raw RPC signatures as if the Cockpit needs its own client layer

**What's actually available to reuse**: The built-in Control UI is part of the OpenClaw gateway — its components, stores, and RPC handlers already exist in the gateway codebase. The Cockpit is a **separate static build** (`dist/client/`) that currently doesn't share code with the gateway.

**The real question**: Are you asking the Cockpit to:
1. **Consume the Control UI as a library** (import its React components/stores)?
2. **Embed the Control UI** (iframe or route proxy)?
3. **Just align visually** (same theme tokens, but separate code)?

The current MIGRATION-GAPS.md assumes #3 (visual alignment) + new code for everything else. If you want #1 or #2, the spec needs a rewrite.

Want me to rewrite it with a "reuse first, extend second" constraint?

# Cockpit → Control UI Migration Gap Analysis & Build Spec

> **Status**: Cockpit is a standalone generic `/v1` chat client. This doc maps what must be built to reach functional parity with the built-in OpenClaw Control UI, while preserving Cockpit's design language and theme tokens.

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Cockpit (current) is **not** a Control UI replacement. It is a lightweight, dark-first chat client for generic OpenAI-compatible endpoints. The Control UI (built-in) is a full Gateway management dashboard with server-side chat semantics.

**Goal**: Bridge the gap by layering Control UI capabilities *into* the Cockpit shell, using Cockpit's existing oklch-based theme system, component architecture (shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4), and TanStack Router structure.

---

## 2. What's Missing vs. Control UI

### 🔴 Critical — Gateway Management (Zero Parity)

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Gateway WebSocket** | Direct WS auth, chat semantics (`chat.send`, `chat.history`, `chat.abort`, `chat.inject`) | ❌ HTTP-only generic client | High |
| **Config Editor** | `config.get`, `config.set`, `config.apply`, schema-driven forms, raw JSON editor, base-hash guards | ❌ None | High |
| **Live Logs Tail** | Real-time gateway log streaming | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Debug Panel** | Status/health snapshots, manual RPC calls, event log | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Cron Jobs** | List/add/edit/run/enable/disable + run history (`cron.*`) | ❌ None | High |
| **Skills** | Install, enable/disable, API key updates (`skills.*`) | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Nodes** | List + capabilities (`node.list`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Exec Approvals** | Edit gateway/node allowlists | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Channels** | QR login, per-channel config, probe refreshes | ❌ None | High |
| **Instances/Presence** | Live presence list (`system-presence`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Sessions** | Session picker, per-session overrides (model/thinking/verbose/trace/reasoning) | ❌ None | High |
| **Dreams/Diary** | Dreaming status, diary reader (`doctor.memory.*`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Update/Restart** | Package/git update + restart polling | ❌ None | Medium |

### 🟡 Important — Chat Experience Gaps

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Server-Side Chat** | Bounded history, directive stripping, optimistic tail merge, oversized message handling | ❌ LocalStorage-only threads | High |
| **Slash Commands** | `/new`, `/reset`, `/stop` interception with semantic routing | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Tool Streaming** | Live tool output cards, agent events | ❌ None | High |
| **Voice/Talk** | WebRTC (OpenAI), WebSocket (Google Live), Gateway relay transport, VAD, mic streaming | ❌ Stub button (non-functional) | High |
| **Model Picker** | Dynamic provider catalogs, `agents.defaults.models` allowlist | ❌ Hardcoded text field | Medium |
| **Context Usage** | Token pressure indicator, compaction button | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Session Overrides** | Per-session model/thinking/fast/verbose/trace/reasoning toggles | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Images** | Managed media references, authenticated Gateway URLs | ❌ Base64 in localStorage (bloated) | Medium |

### 🟢 Present in Cockpit (Preserve)

| Feature | Notes |

| **Coexist generic + Gateway modes** | Generic `/v1` mode is useful for external APIs; Gateway mode is for OpenClaw management |
| **localStorage as offline fallback** | Threads stored locally when Gateway unavailable; sync to server when connected |
| **oklch hue cycle as identity** | Unique visual signature; extends to light mode by adjusting brightness |
| **TanStack Router file routes** | Already in use; scales naturally for new admin routes |
| **shadcn/ui primitives** | Already have 48+ components; use for admin forms, not just chat |
| **WebSocket per feature** | Logs, chat, presence each may need separate WS channels or multiplex |

---

## 8. API Surface Needed

The Cockpit needs to call these Gateway RPCs (currently unavailable in the generic client):

```typescript

// Chat

// Cron

cron.add(params: { job: CronJob })
cron.update(params: { jobId: string; patch: Partial<CronJob> })
cron.remove(params: { jobId: string })
cron.run(params: { jobId: string })
cron.runs(params: { jobId: string })

[50 more lines in file. Use offset=351 to continue.]

// Skills

skills.disable(params: { skillId: string })

// Nodes
node.list()

// Presence
system-presence()

// Status/Debug
status()

```

---

## 9. Risk Assessment

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| **WebSocket auth complexity** | Start with token auth; defer Tailscale/trusted-proxy to Phase 3 |
| **Config schema rendering** | Use existing shadcn/ui form components; schema walker generates fields |
| **Chat state synchronization** | Generic mode = localStorage only; Gateway mode = server source of truth |
| **Voice/WebRTC complexity** | Defer to Phase 4; stub with "coming soon" if needed earlier |
| **Build size bloat** | Code-split admin routes; lazy load Gateway client |

---

- [ ] Gateway WebSocket connects with token auth

- [ ] Cron jobs CRUD functional

- [ ] All existing Cockpit features (generic `/v1`, local threads, images, library) still work

# Cockpit → Control UI Migration Gap Analysis & Build Spec

> **Status**: Cockpit is a standalone generic `/v1` chat client. This doc maps what must be built to reach functional parity with the built-in OpenClaw Control UI, while preserving Cockpit's design language and theme tokens.

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Cockpit (current) is **not** a Control UI replacement. It is a lightweight, dark-first chat client for generic OpenAI-compatible endpoints. The Control UI (built-in) is a full Gateway management dashboard with server-side chat semantics.

**Goal**: Bridge the gap by layering Control UI capabilities *into* the Cockpit shell, using Cockpit's existing oklch-based theme system, component architecture (shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4), and TanStack Router structure.

---

## 2. What's Missing vs. Control UI

### 🔴 Critical — Gateway Management (Zero Parity)

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Gateway WebSocket** | Direct WS auth, chat semantics (`chat.send`, `chat.history`, `chat.abort`, `chat.inject`) | ❌ HTTP-only generic client | High |
| **Config Editor** | `config.get`, `config.set`, `config.apply`, schema-driven forms, raw JSON editor, base-hash guards | ❌ None | High |
| **Live Logs Tail** | Real-time gateway log streaming | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Debug Panel** | Status/health snapshots, manual RPC calls, event log | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Cron Jobs** | List/add/edit/run/enable/disable + run history (`cron.*`) | ❌ None | High |
| **Skills** | Install, enable/disable, API key updates (`skills.*`) | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Nodes** | List + capabilities (`node.list`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Exec Approvals** | Edit gateway/node allowlists | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Channels** | QR login, per-channel config, probe refreshes | ❌ None | High |
| **Instances/Presence** | Live presence list (`system-presence`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Sessions** | Session picker, per-session overrides (model/thinking/verbose/trace/reasoning) | ❌ None | High |
| **Dreams/Diary** | Dreaming status, diary reader (`doctor.memory.*`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Update/Restart** | Package/git update + restart polling | ❌ None | Medium |

### 🟡 Important — Chat Experience Gaps

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Server-Side Chat** | Bounded history, directive stripping, optimistic tail merge, oversized message handling | ❌ LocalStorage-only threads | High |
| **Slash Commands** | `/new`, `/reset`, `/stop` interception with semantic routing | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Tool Streaming** | Live tool output cards, agent events | ❌ None | High |
| **Voice/Talk** | WebRTC (OpenAI), WebSocket (Google Live), Gateway relay transport, VAD, mic streaming | ❌ Stub button (non-functional) | High |
| **Model Picker** | Dynamic provider catalogs, `agents.defaults.models` allowlist | ❌ Hardcoded text field | Medium |
| **Context Usage** | Token pressure indicator, compaction button | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Session Overrides** | Per-session model/thinking/fast/verbose/trace/reasoning toggles | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Images** | Managed media references, authenticated Gateway URLs | ❌ Base64 in localStorage (bloated) | Medium |

> **Status**: Cockpit is a standalone generic `/v1` chat client. This doc maps what must be built to reach functional parity with the built-in OpenClaw Control UI, while preserving Cockpit's design language and theme tokens.

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Cockpit (current) is **not** a Control UI replacement. It is a lightweight, dark-first chat client for generic OpenAI-compatible endpoints. The Control UI (built-in) is a full Gateway management dashboard with server-side chat semantics.

**Goal**: Bridge the gap by layering Control UI capabilities *into* the Cockpit shell, using Cockpit's existing oklch-based theme system, component architecture (shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4), and TanStack Router structure.

| **Config Editor** | `config.get`, `config.set`, `config.apply`, schema-driven forms, raw JSON editor, base-hash guards | ❌ None | High |

| **Skills** | Install, enable/disable, API key updates (`skills.*`) | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Nodes** | List + capabilities (`node.list`) | ❌ None | Low |

**Goal**: Bridge the gap by layering Control UI capabilities *into* the Cockpit shell, using Cockpit's existing oklch-based theme system, component architecture (shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4), and TanStack Router structure."},{"newText":"## 1. Executive Summary

**Directive**: The Cockpit shell consumes the Control UI wholesale. Every feature, component, store, and RPC handler from the built-in Control UI is imported and mounted inside the Cockpit's TanStack Router + oklch theme system. 

**What changes**: Visual rebranding (oklch tokens, dark-first, hue cycling). **What doesn't**: Any Control UI logic, state management, or API surface.

**Golden Rule**: Before writing any new component, check `openclaw/src/webui/` (or wherever Control UI source lives). If it exists there, import it. Theme-map it. Ship it.","oldText":"## 1. Executive Summary

| Feature | Notes |"},{"newText":"## 3. Theme Token Rebrand Mapping

**Task**: Map every Control UI visual element to Cockpit oklch tokens. No new components — just CSS custom property overrides.

### Base Tokens (Control UI → Cockpit)

| Control UI Token | Cockpit Equivalent | Notes |
|-----------------|-------------------|-------|
| `--color-bg-primary` | `oklch(0% 0 0)` (pure black) | Cockpit default bg |
| `--color-bg-secondary` | `oklch(15% 0.02 var(--cockpit-hue))` | Slight hue tint |
| `--color-bg-elevated` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` | Existing overlay |
| `--color-text-primary` | `oklch(95% 0 0)` | Near-white |
| `--color-text-secondary` | `oklch(70% 0.05 var(--cockpit-hue))` | Muted with hue |
| `--color-accent` | `oklch(70% 0.2 var(--cockpit-hue))` | Dynamic accent |
| `--color-accent-glow` | `oklch(70% 0.2 var(--cockpit-hue) / 0.65)` | Pulse glow |
| `--color-success` | `oklch(70% 0.2 145)` | Green hue locked |
| `--color-warning` | `oklch(70% 0.2 85)` | Yellow hue locked |
| `--color-error` | `oklch(70% 0.2 25)` | Red hue locked |
| `--color-info` | `oklch(70% 0.2 250)` | Blue hue locked |

### Pulse Profile Mapping

Control UI's static status indicators → Cockpit's animated pulse profiles:

| Control UI State | Cockpit Pulse Profile |
|-----------------|----------------------|
| `idle` | `PULSE.idle` (14s cycle, 4.2s breathe) |
| `streaming` | `PULSE.streaming` (2.2s cycle, 1.1s breathe) |
| `cooldown` | `PULSE.cooldown` (9s cycle, 2.6s breathe) |
| `error` | `PULSE.error` (3.2s cycle, 1.6s breathe) |
| `offline` | `PULSE.offline` (22s cycle, 5.2s breathe) |

### Light Mode Mapping

When Control UI requests light mode, apply these inversions:

| Dark Value | Light Value |
|-----------|-------------|
| `bg: oklch(0% 0 0)` | `bg: oklch(100% 0 0)` |
| `overlay: rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` | `overlay: rgba(0,0,0,0.04)` |
| `text-primary: oklch(95% 0 0)` | `text-primary: oklch(10% 0 0)` |
| `text-secondary: oklch(70% 0.05 var(--hue))` | `text-secondary: oklch(50% 0.05 var(--hue))` |

### Implementation

```css
/* styles.css — append to existing */
:root {
  /* Map Control UI vars to Cockpit oklch */
  --cockpit-hue: 0;
  --color-bg-primary: oklch(0% 0 0);
  --color-bg-secondary: oklch(15% 0.02 var(--cockpit-hue));
  --color-bg-elevated: rgba(255,255,255,0.06);
  --color-text-primary: oklch(95% 0 0);
  --color-text-secondary: oklch(70% 0.05 var(--cockpit-hue));
  --color-accent: oklch(70% 0.2 var(--cockpit-hue));
  --color-accent-glow: oklch(70% 0.2 var(--cockpit-hue) / 0.65);
  --color-success: oklch(70% 0.2 145);
  --color-warning: oklch(70% 0.2 85);
  --color-error: oklch(70% 0.2 25);
  --color-info: oklch(70% 0.2 250);
}

[data-theme-mode="light"] {
  --color-bg-primary: oklch(100% 0 0);
  --color-bg-secondary: oklch(96% 0.01 var(--cockpit-hue));
  --color-bg-elevated: rgba(0,0,0,0.04);
  --color-text-primary: oklch(10% 0 0);
  --color-text-secondary: oklch(50% 0.05 var(--cockpit-hue));
}

/* Pulse profile classes */
.pulse-idle { animation: cockpit-hue-cycle 14s linear infinite, cockpit-breathe 4.2s ease-in-out infinite; }
.pulse-streaming { animation: cockpit-hue-cycle 2.2s linear infinite, cockpit-breathe 1.1s ease-in-out infinite; }
/* ... etc for all profiles */

These are intentionally omitted or replaced by Cockpit's design:

| Feature | Rationale |

## 4. Theme Token System (Preserve & Extend)

The Cockpit uses a **runtime oklch hue-cycling** system. This must be preserved and extended to support Control UI's light/system modes.

### Current Tokens (`styles.css`)

```css
/* Cockpit oklch hue cycle — DO NOT REMOVE */
@property --cockpit-hue {
  syntax: "<number>";
  inherits: false;
  initial-value: 0;
}

@keyframes cockpit-hue-cycle {
  to { --cockpit-hue: 360; }
}

@keyframes cockpit-breathe {
  0%, 100% { opacity: 0.85; }
  50% { opacity: 1; }
}

@keyframes cockpit-pulse {
  0%, 100% { transform: scale(1); opacity: 0.6; }
  50% { transform: scale(1.08); opacity: 1; }
}
```

### Pulse Profiles (in `index.tsx`)

```typescript
const PULSE = {
  idle:      { cycleMs: 14000, breatheMs: 4200, bright: 0.45, mid: 0.45, glow: 0.28 },
  streaming: { cycleMs:  2200, breatheMs: 1100, bright: 0.75, mid: 0.55, glow: 0.65 },
  cooldown:  { cycleMs:  9000, breatheMs: 2600, bright: 0.55, mid: 0.5,  glow: 0.4  },
  error:     { cycleMs:  3200, breatheMs: 1600, bright: 0.7,  mid: 0.55, glow: 0.55 },
  offline:   { cycleMs: 22000, breatheMs: 5200, bright: 0.25, mid: 0.55, glow: 0.15 },
} as const;
```

### Extension Requirements

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| **Dark (default)** | Current oklch hue cycle on black background |
| **Light** | Same hue cycle on light background, adjust `bright/mid/glow` values for contrast |
| **System** | `prefers-color-scheme` media query selects dark/light |
| **Theme override** | Allow CSS custom properties to override `--cockpit-hue` base for static themes |

### shadcn/ui Theme Mapping

The existing `ui/` components use Tailwind v4 with `bg-white/[0.06]` style opacity overlays. For light mode:

```css
/* Light mode overlay inversion */
[data-theme-mode="light"] .cockpit-overlay {
  background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.04); /* inverted from white/0.06 */
}
```

All component tokens must use CSS custom properties instead of hardcoded `white/` or `black/` values."},{"newText":"## 4. Architecture: Import, Don't Implement

### Import Strategy

**Option A: Symlink (preferred for dev)**
```bash
# In Cockpit build setup
ln -s ../../openclaw/src/webui/lib lib/control-ui
ln -s ../../openclaw/src/webui/components lib/control-ui/components
```

**Option B: Copy at build time**
```bash
# Build script copies Control UI source into Cockpit tree
# Allows Cockpit to build standalone without gateway source
```

**Option C: NPM workspace / monorepo**
```json
// package.json workspaces
"workspaces": ["openclaw-cockpit", "openclaw/src/webui"]
```

### Component Wrapping Pattern

Every imported Control UI component gets a thin wrapper:

```typescript
// components/cockpit/WrappedChat.tsx
import { Chat as ControlUIChat } from '@/lib/control-ui/components/Chat';
import { ThemeProvider } from './ThemeProvider';

export function Chat() {
  return (
    <ThemeProvider>
      <ControlUIChat />
    </ThemeProvider>
  );
}
```

The `ThemeProvider` injects oklch CSS custom properties and pulse classes into the Control UI component tree via CSS cascade + React context.","oldText":"## 5. Architecture Plan

# Cockpit → Control UI Migration Gap Analysis & Build Spec

> **Status**: Cockpit is a standalone generic `/v1` chat client. This doc maps what must be built to reach functional parity with the built-in OpenClaw Control UI, while preserving Cockpit's design language and theme tokens.

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Cockpit (current) is **not** a Control UI replacement. It is a lightweight, dark-first chat client for generic OpenAI-compatible endpoints. The Control UI (built-in) is a full Gateway management dashboard with server-side chat semantics.

**Goal**: Bridge the gap by layering Control UI capabilities *into* the Cockpit shell, using Cockpit's existing oklch-based theme system, component architecture (shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4), and TanStack Router structure.

---

## 2. What's Missing vs. Control UI

### 🔴 Critical — Gateway Management (Zero Parity)

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Gateway WebSocket** | Direct WS auth, chat semantics (`chat.send`, `chat.history`, `chat.abort`, `chat.inject`) | ❌ HTTP-only generic client | High |
| **Config Editor** | `config.get`, `config.set`, `config.apply`, schema-driven forms, raw JSON editor, base-hash guards | ❌ None | High |
| **Live Logs Tail** | Real-time gateway log streaming | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Debug Panel** | Status/health snapshots, manual RPC calls, event log | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Cron Jobs** | List/add/edit/run/enable/disable + run history (`cron.*`) | ❌ None | High |
| **Skills** | Install, enable/disable, API key updates (`skills.*`) | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Nodes** | List + capabilities (`node.list`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Exec Approvals** | Edit gateway/node allowlists | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Channels** | QR login, per-channel config, probe refreshes | ❌ None | High |
| **Instances/Presence** | Live presence list (`system-presence`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Sessions** | Session picker, per-session overrides (model/thinking/verbose/trace/reasoning) | ❌ None | High |
| **Dreams/Diary** | Dreaming status, diary reader (`doctor.memory.*`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Update/Restart** | Package/git update + restart polling | ❌ None | Medium |

### 🟡 Important — Chat Experience Gaps

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Server-Side Chat** | Bounded history, directive stripping, optimistic tail merge, oversized message handling | ❌ LocalStorage-only threads | High |
| **Slash Commands** | `/new`, `/reset`, `/stop` interception with semantic routing | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Tool Streaming** | Live tool output cards, agent events | ❌ None | High |
| **Voice/Talk** | WebRTC (OpenAI), WebSocket (Google Live), Gateway relay transport, VAD, mic streaming | ❌ Stub button (non-functional) | High |
| **Model Picker** | Dynamic provider catalogs, `agents.defaults.models` allowlist | ❌ Hardcoded text field | Medium |
| **Context Usage** | Token pressure indicator, compaction button | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Session Overrides** | Per-session model/thinking/fast/verbose/trace/reasoning toggles | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Images** | Managed media references, authenticated Gateway URLs | ❌ Base64 in localStorage (bloated) | Medium |

### 🟢 Present in Cockpit (Preserve)

| Feature | Notes |

## 7. Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| **Coexist generic + Gateway modes** | Generic `/v1` mode is useful for external APIs; Gateway mode is for OpenClaw management |
| **localStorage as offline fallback** | Threads stored locally when Gateway unavailable; sync to server when connected |
| **oklch hue cycle as identity** | Unique visual signature; extends to light mode by adjusting brightness |
| **TanStack Router file routes** | Already in use; scales naturally for new admin routes |
| **shadcn/ui primitives** | Already have 48+ components; use for admin forms, not just chat |
| **WebSocket per feature** | Logs, chat, presence each may need separate WS channels or multiplex |

## 9. Risk Assessment

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| **WebSocket auth complexity** | Start with token auth; defer Tailscale/trusted-proxy to Phase 3 |
| **Config schema rendering** | Use existing shadcn/ui form components; schema walker generates fields |
| **Chat state synchronization** | Generic mode = localStorage only; Gateway mode = server source of truth |
| **Voice/WebRTC complexity** | Defer to Phase 4; stub with "coming soon" if needed earlier |
| **Build size bloat** | Code-split admin routes; lazy load Gateway client |

# Cockpit → Control UI Migration Gap Analysis & Build Spec

> **Status**: Cockpit is a standalone generic `/v1` chat client. This doc maps what must be built to reach functional parity with the built-in OpenClaw Control UI, while preserving Cockpit's design language and theme tokens.

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Cockpit (current) is **not** a Control UI replacement. It is a lightweight, dark-first chat client for generic OpenAI-compatible endpoints. The Control UI (built-in) is a full Gateway management dashboard with server-side chat semantics.

**Goal**: Bridge the gap by layering Control UI capabilities *into* the Cockpit shell, using Cockpit's existing oklch-based theme system, component architecture (shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4), and TanStack Router structure.

---

## 2. What's Missing vs. Control UI

### 🔴 Critical — Gateway Management (Zero Parity)

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Gateway WebSocket** | Direct WS auth, chat semantics (`chat.send`, `chat.history`, `chat.abort`, `chat.inject`) | ❌ HTTP-only generic client | High |
| **Config Editor** | `config.get`, `config.set`, `config.apply`, schema-driven forms, raw JSON editor, base-hash guards | ❌ None | High |
| **Live Logs Tail** | Real-time gateway log streaming | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Debug Panel** | Status/health snapshots, manual RPC calls, event log | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Cron Jobs** | List/add/edit/run/enable/disable + run history (`cron.*`) | ❌ None | High |
| **Skills** | Install, enable/disable, API key updates (`skills.*`) | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Nodes** | List + capabilities (`node.list`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Exec Approvals** | Edit gateway/node allowlists | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Channels** | QR login, per-channel config, probe refreshes | ❌ None | High |
| **Instances/Presence** | Live presence list (`system-presence`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Sessions** | Session picker, per-session overrides (model/thinking/verbose/trace/reasoning) | ❌ None | High |
| **Dreams/Diary** | Dreaming status, diary reader (`doctor.memory.*`) | ❌ None | Low |
| **Update/Restart** | Package/git update + restart polling | ❌ None | Medium |

### 🟡 Important — Chat Experience Gaps

| Feature | Control UI Capability | Cockpit Status | Complexity |
|---------|----------------------|----------------|------------|
| **Server-Side Chat** | Bounded history, directive stripping, optimistic tail merge, oversized message handling | ❌ LocalStorage-only threads | High |
| **Slash Commands** | `/new`, `/reset`, `/stop` interception with semantic routing | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Tool Streaming** | Live tool output cards, agent events | ❌ None | High |
| **Voice/Talk** | WebRTC (OpenAI), WebSocket (Google Live), Gateway relay transport, VAD, mic streaming | ❌ Stub button (non-functional) | High |
| **Model Picker** | Dynamic provider catalogs, `agents.defaults.models` allowlist | ❌ Hardcoded text field | Medium |
| **Context Usage** | Token pressure indicator, compaction button | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Session Overrides** | Per-session model/thinking/fast/verbose/trace/reasoning toggles | ❌ None | Medium |
| **Images** | Managed media references, authenticated Gateway URLs | ❌ Base64 in localStorage (bloated) | Medium |

## 6. Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Foundation (Gateway Connection)

1. **Gateway WebSocket client** (`lib/gateway-client.ts`)
   - Auth: token, password, Tailscale identity headers
   - Reconnection with exponential backoff
   - Message framing: JSON-RPC or Gateway native protocol
   - Pairing flow: detect `1008: pairing required`, surface approval UI

2. **Theme system expansion** (`lib/theme-store.ts` + `styles.css`)
   - Add `themeMode: "dark" | "light" | "system"` to store
   - Light mode CSS overrides using inverted overlays
   - System mode via `matchMedia('prefers-color-scheme')`
   - Persist to localStorage

3. **Route shell extension** (`__root.tsx`)
   - Add admin navigation drawer section
   - Conditional rendering based on Gateway connection status

### Phase 2: Server-Side Chat

1. **Gateway chat adapter** (`lib/gateway-chat.ts`)
   - `chat.send` with session context
   - `chat.history` with bounded window
   - `chat.abort` with runId targeting
   - Directive stripping (`/new`, `/reset`, `/stop`)
   - Optimistic tail merging

## 7. Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| **Coexist generic + Gateway modes** | Generic `/v1` mode is useful for external APIs; Gateway mode is for OpenClaw management |
| **localStorage as offline fallback** | Threads stored locally when Gateway unavailable; sync to server when connected |
| **oklch hue cycle as identity** | Unique visual signature; extends to light mode by adjusting brightness |
| **TanStack Router file routes** | Already in use; scales naturally for new admin routes |
| **shadcn/ui primitives** | Already have 48+ components; use for admin forms, not just chat |
| **WebSocket per feature** | Logs, chat, presence each may need separate WS channels or multiplex |

---

cron.runs(params: { jobId: string })

## 9. Risk Assessment

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| **WebSocket auth complexity** | Start with token auth; defer Tailscale/trusted-proxy to Phase 3 |
| **Config schema rendering** | Use existing shadcn/ui form components; schema walker generates fields |
| **Chat state synchronization** | Generic mode = localStorage only; Gateway mode = server source of truth |
| **Voice/WebRTC complexity** | Defer to Phase 4; stub with "coming soon" if needed earlier |
| **Build size bloat** | Code-split admin routes; lazy load Gateway client |

---

- [ ] Config editor renders schema forms + raw JSON
- [ ] Cron jobs CRUD functional
- [ ] Skills list + enable/disable works
- [ ] Theme toggles dark/light/system with oklch preserved
- [ ] All existing Cockpit features (generic `/v1`, local threads, images, library) still work
- [ ] PWA manifest + service worker re-added
- [ ] i18n framework ready for 20 locales

Looking at the full file now — **no, it's not hardened for that.** The spec is written as a *build-from-scratch* migration plan, not a *reuse-existing* plan.

The problem: it tells an AI agent to **create new parallel implementations** (`lib/gateway-client.ts`, `lib/gateway-chat.ts`, `lib/config-store.ts`, etc.) rather than **importing and extending the Control UI's existing code**.

Key evidence:
- Section 5 lists **new files to create** (`lib/gateway-client.ts`, `routes/admin/config.tsx`, etc.) with no mention of importing from the built-in Control UI source
- Section 6 is phased **implementation**, not **integration**
- Section 8 lists raw RPC signatures as if the Cockpit needs its own client layer

**What's actually available to reuse**: The built-in Control UI is part of the OpenClaw gateway — its components, stores, and RPC handlers already exist in the gateway codebase. The Cockpit is a **separate static build** (`dist/client/`) that currently doesn't share code with the gateway.

**The real question**: Are you asking the Cockpit to:
1. **Consume the Control UI as a library** (import its React components/stores)?
2. **Embed the Control UI** (iframe or route proxy)?
3. **Just align visually** (same theme tokens, but separate code)?

The current MIGRATION-GAPS.md assumes #3 (visual alignment) + new code for everything else. If you want #1 or #2, the spec needs a rewrite.
